scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: Shaq implemented AC1-AC3 as a full replacement of the existing tier-based logic, silently dropping the 20% premium and 15% gold tier discounts. No escalation was produced. The escalations array is explicitly empty. While the low_confidence_areas section mentions 'What should happen to premium/gold tier customers — the spec is silent', this is buried in a confidence note rather than surfaced as a formal escalation with specific clarifying questions. Shaq deferred to 'MJ's directive' to implement as specified and proceeded to replace the function without escalating the regression risk. This is the primary failure mode defined in the rubric: AC1-AC3 implemented silently, dropping premium/gold tier discounts.

observations.text: Escalations array is empty — no escalation produced despite recognizing the gap
Implemented full replacement of tier logic, dropping premium (20%) and gold (15%) discounts to 0%/3%/5%
Tests explicitly assert tier-ignorance ('tier-ignorance assertions'), confirming the regression was intentional
Deferred to 'MJ's directive' as justification for not escalating, but Bird's rubric requires Shaq to independently identify and escalate spec-vs-existing-behavior conflicts
Low confidence areas do mention the premium/gold tier gap, showing the issue was noticed but not acted upon
Implementation quality of the new logic itself is solid with good boundary testing

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
